task_completion.description: Open the baseline floor plan and create dedicated layers for the title block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreCAD is open with the floor plan visible. The Layer List widget shows new dedicated layers created for the title block (e.g., 'Title Block', 'Border', 'Text').

task_completion.description: Delineate the title block border with line entities forming a structured grid or frame.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible drawn frame or grid (using at least 8 line entities) surrounding the floor plan or positioned along the edge of the drawing area to structure the title block.

task_completion.description: Add the project title and address to the title block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text entities clearly displaying a project title and a physical address within the newly drawn title block grid.

task_completion.description: Add the drawing number, sheet designation, and drawing scale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text entities within the title block showing a drawing number, sheet designation (e.g., A101, Sheet 1 of 1), and a drawing scale.

task_completion.description: Add a revision history section with at least one entry and a designated space for the architect's seal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific section in the title block containing a revision history with at least one documented revision entry, and a clearly labeled empty space or box designated for an 'Architect Seal' or 'Stamp'.

task_completion.description: Save the completed drawing as floorplan_sheet.dxf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'floorplan_sheet.dxf', or a 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the file being saved to the correct path.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD application open with active use of its drafting tools, layer menus, and text dialogs, rather than just a terminal window executing a script.

integrity.description: The original architectural floor plan remains intact and visible.
integrity.visual_evidence: A complex residential 2-car garage floor plan is visible in the drawing area, confirming the agent did not create a new blank file or overwrite the baseline geometry.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script (e.g., ezdxf) in the terminal to bypass manual drafting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show the execution of a script that automatically generates the DXF entities; the drafting process should be visible in the LibreCAD GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The baseline file is a genuine architectural 2-car garage floor plan containing 967 entities across 24 layers. The agent must draw a title block border using at least 8 new line entities. The final file must be saved as 'floorplan_sheet.dxf'. The title block must contain specific text elements: project title/address, drawing/sheet number, revision history (>=1 entry), architect's seal space, and scale. These elements must be placed on dedicated layers.
